methods.description: This method determines the particle-size distribution of soil samples using a laser analyzer, which helps in understanding the soil texture and composition.

steps.description: Samples were pre-treated with an ultrasonic disruptor.
steps.searchable_summary: pre-treat soil samples using ultrasonic disruptor

steps.description: Particle-size distribution was determined using an Analysette 22 Comfort laser analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze particle-size distribution with laser analyzer

methods.description: This method involves preparing thin sections of soil samples for micromorphological analysis to study soil structure and composition.

steps.description: Thin sections for micromorphology were prepared using a polyester resin, cut and polished to obtain a 30 μm section.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare thin sections of soil samples

steps.description: Micromorphological descriptions were made using an Olympus petrographic microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze thin sections with petrographic microscope

methods.description: This method measures the magnetic susceptibility of soil samples to differentiate between pedogenic and lithogenic processes.

steps.description: Each sample was air dried, gently crushed, and packed into cubical diamagnetic boxes.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare soil samples for magnetic susceptibility measurement

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility was measured with a Bartington MS2B susceptibility meter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure magnetic susceptibility with susceptibility meter

methods.description: This method analyzes the bulk chemical composition of soil samples to determine the concentration of major and trace elements.

steps.description: 5 g of soil were crushed and sieved, then placed in plastic bags.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare soil samples for chemical analysis

steps.description: Measurements were made using a NITON XL3t Thermo Scientific portable analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze chemical composition with portable analyzer

methods.description: This method uses computed tomography to study the pore distribution and connectivity in soil samples.

steps.description: Tomographic scanning of soil samples was performed using a Bruker SkyScan 1172 G 3D X-ray scanner.
steps.searchable_summary: perform tomographic scanning of soil samples

steps.description: Image reconstruction was performed using Bruker nRecon software.
Morphometric analysis was performed using Bruker CTAn software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform morphometric analysis on reconstructed images

methods.description: This method studies the detritus, phytoliths, sponge spicules, and other remains of biomorphs in soil samples.

steps.description: A 50 g sample was treated with a hot 30% solution of H2O2.
steps.searchable_summary: treat soil sample with H2O2 solution

steps.description: Sand and clay fractions were separated from silt, which was subjected to flotation in a heavy liquid.
steps.searchable_summary: separate soil fractions and perform flotation

steps.description: The floating siliceous particles and other biomorphs were collected, washed, dried, and studied under the optical microscope.
